sections.paragraph: (for רְנֹון “rushing,” “roaring,” i.e. roaring stream), pr.n. of a stream (נַחַל) with a valley of the same name, emptying itself into the east of the Dead Sea; it formerly was the northern boundary of the Moabites, the southern of the Amorites (now المو جب el-Môjib). Num 21:13, seq.; 22:36 Deu 2:24, 36 3:8 , seq.; 4:48 Isa 16:2, and see Burckhardt, Travels in Syria, p. 372 (Germ. trans. p. 633), and my remarks on Isa 16:2.
